description:
You are an Interplanetary Mission Analyst at JPL. Calculate the theoretical delta-V savings achieved by aerobraking the MarsExplorer spacecraft (1200 kg, 35 m^2 drag area, Cd 2.2) in the Martian atmosphere for 60 days compared to a propulsive retrograde burn. Use the mission specifications and Vis-Viva formulas provided in ~/Desktop/aerobraking_spec.txt. The simulation must use a Mars-centered Force Model with Point Mass gravity (Degree 0, Order 0) and the Mars Exponential atmosphere model to isolate drag effects. Determine the equivalent retrograde burn at the final periapsis required to achieve the same apoapsis reduction as the 60-day aerobraking phase. Save the simulation script to ~/Documents/missions/mars_aerobraking.script and write a report to ~/GMAT_output/aerobraking_results.txt containing the following lines:
initial_apoapsis_radius_km: 23396.19
final_periapsis_radius_km: [value]
final_apoapsis_radius_km: [value]
deltav_saved_mps: [value]

Login: username=ga, password=password123
